Fear is stupid. So are regrets. — Marilyn Monroe
Man should fear never beginning to live. — Marcus Aurelius
Action may not always bring happiness; but there is no happiness without action. — Benjamin Disraeli
Absence makes the heart grow fonder. — Thomas Haynes Bayly
Many sophisticated, intelligent people lack wisdom and common sense. — Joyce Meyer
People are so constituted that everybody would rather undertake what they see others do, whether they have an aptitude for it or not. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
In action a great heart is the chief qualification. In work, a great head. — Arthur Schopenhauer
Living a life that has a greater purpose and living up to it is the ultimate degree that we should strive for. — Gurbaksh Chahal
Take things as they are. Punch when you have to punch. Kick when you have to kick. — Bruce Lee
If you only knew the magnificence of the 3, 6, and 9, then you would have a key to the universe. — Nikola Tesla
Being deeply loved by someone gives you strength, while loving someone deeply gives you courage. — Lao Tzu
If you do not express your own original ideas, if you do not listen to your own being, you will have betrayed yourself. — Rollo May
Life is filled with secrets. You can't learn them all at once. — Dan Brown
Engage in those actions and thoughts that nurture the good qualities you want to have. — Paramahansa Yogananda
Motivation is the art of getting people to do what you want them to do because they want to do it. — Dwight D. Eisenhower
No person is your friend who demands your silence or denies your right to grow. — Alice Walker
Forgive those who have hurt you. — Les Brown
Trying to define yourself is like trying to bite your own teeth. — Alan Watts
Wise men talk because they have something to say; fools, because they have to say something. — Plato
Outstanding people have one thing in common: an absolute sense of mission. — Zig Ziglar
The least of things with a meaning is worth more in life than the greatest of things without it. — Carl Jung
We don't stop going to school when we graduate. — Carol Burnett
Who you are is defined by what you're willing to struggle for. — Mark Manson
It's the job that's never started that takes the longest to finish. — J.R.R. Tolkien
When a thing is done, it's done. Don't look back. Look forward to your next objective. — George Marshall
We shape clay into a pot, but it is the emptiness inside that holds whatever we want. — Lao Tzu
Your worst enemy cannot harm you as much as your own unguarded thoughts. — The Buddha
The biggest adventure you can ever take is to live the life of your dreams. — Oprah Winfrey
Focused, hard work is the real key to success. — John Carmack
Nothing could be worse than the fear that one had given up too soon and left one unexpended effort that might have saved the world. — Jane Addams
He is a wise man who does not grieve for the things which he has not but rejoices for those which he has. — Epictetus
It is impossible for a man to learn what he thinks he already knows. — Epictetus
He who has imagination without learning has wings but no feet. — Joseph Joubert
Discontent is the first necessity of progress. — Thomas Edison
Friendship is like money, easier made than kept. — Samuel Butler
To free us from the expectations of others, to give us back to ourselves... there lies the great, singular power of self-respect. — Joan Didion
Maxim for life: You get treated in life the way you teach people to treat you. — Wayne Dyer
It is ignorance that causes most mistakes. — Harry S. Truman
Technology is a word that describes something that doesn't work yet. — Douglas Adams
To study and not think is a waste. To think and not study is dangerous. — Confucius
You are that vast thing that you see far, far off with great telescopes. — Alan Watts
Some of your greatest lessons come from your darkest moments. — Roger Lee
They must often change, who would be constant in happiness or wisdom. — Confucius
Love is a friendship set to music. — Joseph Campbell
Accept challenges, so that you may feel the exhilaration of victory. — George S. Patton
The meaning I picked, the one that changed my life: Overcome fear, behold wonder. — Richard Bach
Your mind will answer most questions if you learn to relax and wait for the answer. — William Burroughs
We read the world wrong and say that it deceives us. — Rabindranath Tagore
Love is the energy of life. — Robert Browning
No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now. — Alan Watts
Most great people have attained their greatest success just one step beyond their greatest failure. — Napoleon Hill
Do not look for a sanctuary in anyone except your self. — Buddha
All that a man achieves and all that he fails to achieve is the direct result of his own thoughts. — James Allen
All great men are gifted with intuition. They know without reasoning or analysis, what they need to know. — Alexis Carrel
I believe that a simple and unassuming manner of life is best for everyone, best both for the body and the mind. — Albert Einstein
Let us rather run the risk of wearing out than rusting out. — Theodore Roosevelt
No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man. — Heraclitus
We have a duty to maintain the light of consciousness to make sure it continues into the future. — Elon Musk
Truth, and goodness, and beauty are but different faces of the same all. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Everything you are against weakens you. Everything you are for empowers you. — Wayne Dyer
You can't get to a place that you don't believe exists. — Unknown
He who laughs at himself never runs out of things to laugh at. — Epictetus
Criticism is something you can easily avoid by saying nothing, doing nothing, and being nothing. — Aristotle
If you read a lot of books, you're considered well-read. But if you watch a lot of TV, you're not considered well-viewed. — Lily Tomlin
The best thing in every noble dream is the dreamer... — Moncure D. Conway
A comfort zone is a beautiful place, but nothing ever grows there. — Unknown
It is dangerous to be right in matters on which the established authorities are wrong. — Voltaire
There are two kinds of perfect people: those who are dead, and those who have not been born yet. — Chinese Proverb
The quicker you let go of old cheese, the sooner you find new cheese. — Spencer Johnson
The greatest obstacle to connecting with our joy is resentment. — Pema Chödrön
Often we pass beside happiness without seeing it, without looking at it, or even if we have seen and looked at it, without recognizing it. — Alexandre Dumas
Life is what you make it. Always has been, always will be. — Eleanor Roosevelt
Give me six hours to chop down a tree and I will spend the first four sharpening the axe. — Abraham Lincoln
Nothing can bring you peace but yourself. — Dale Carnegie
Our doubts are traitors and make us lose the good we often might win, by fearing to attempt. — Jane Addams
In order to live free and happily you must sacrifice boredom. It is not always an easy sacrifice. — Richard Bach
Marriage is the highest state of friendship. If happy, it lessens our cares by dividing them, at the same time that it doubles our pleasures by mutual participation. — Samuel Richardson
The measure of who we are is what we do with what we have. — Vince Lombardi
We must learn to live together as brothers or perish together as fools. — Martin Luther King, Jr.
Difficult and meaningful will always bring more satisfaction than easy and meaningless. — Maxime Lagace
Help others achieve their dreams and you will achieve yours. — Les Brown
The path to success is to take massive, determined action. — Tony Robbins
The most technologically efficient machine that man has ever invented is the book. — Northrop Frye
If you hang out with chickens, you're going to cluck and if you hang out with eagles, you're going to fly. — Steve Maraboli
Keep your eyes on the goal, and just keep taking the next step towards completing it. — John Carmack
We ourselves feel that what we are doing is just a drop in the ocean. But the ocean would be less because of that missing drop. — Mother Teresa
The doorstep to the temple of wisdom is a knowledge of our own ignorance. — Benjamin Franklin
I can't listen to that much Wagner. I start getting the urge to conquer Poland. — Woody Allen
Never make friends with people who are above or below you in status. Such friendships will never give you any happiness. — Chanakya
The first step to getting the things you want out of life is this: decide what you want. — Ben Stein
The best teacher is experience and not through someone's distorted point of view. — Jack Kerouac
You want to live but do you know how to live? You are scared of dying and tell me, is the kind of life you lead really any different from being dead? — Seneca
The function of wisdom is to discriminate between good and evil. — Cicero
It is better to fail in originality than to succeed in imitation. — Herman Melville
A mistake is only an error, it becomes a mistake when you fail to correct it. — John Lennon
There is no duty we so underrate as the duty of being happy. By being happy we sow anonymous benefits upon the world. — Robert Louis Stevenson
It is one of the blessings of old friends that you can afford to be stupid with them. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Passion is born deaf and dumb. — Honore de Balzac
A real friend is one who walks in when the rest of the world walks out. — Unknown
Much wisdom often goes with fewest words. — Sophocles
Small men think they are small; great men never know they are great. — Chinese Proverb
That old law about 'an eye for an eye' leaves everybody blind. The time is always right to do the right thing. — Martin Luther King Jr.
Life is much like going to the gym. The most painful part is deciding to go. Once you get past that, it's easy. — Robert Kiyosaki
Until you value yourself, you won't value your time. Until you value your time, you won't do anything with it. — M. Scott Peck
Effort only fully releases its reward after a person refuses to quit. — Napoleon Hill
Although the world is full of suffering, it is also full of the overcoming of it. — Helen Keller
True friendship can afford true knowledge. It does not depend on darkness and ignorance. — Henry David Thoreau
If you are going to achieve excellence in big things, you develop the habit in little matters. — Colin Powell
Never whine, never complain, never try to justify yourself. — Robert Greene
The eyes of others our prisons; their thoughts our cages. — Virginia Woolf
Music is like creating an emotional painting. The sounds are the colors. — Yanni
The Only Thing That Is Constant Is Change — Heraclitus
I am thankful to all those who said no. It's because of them, I did it myself. — Wayne Dyer
The art of storytelling is reaching its end because the epic side of truth, wisdom, is dying out. — Walter Benjamin
A good master leads you to the true master within. Only that master, who is your own higher self, can adequately answer all questions. — Ming-Dao Deng
Memory is the mother of all wisdom. — Samuel Johnson
Ideas do not reach perfection in a day, no matter how much study is put upon them. — Alexander Graham Bell
It is amazing what you can accomplish if you do not care who gets the credit. — Harry S. Truman
One's friends are that part of the human race with which one can be human. — George Santayana
Behind every beautiful thing, there's some kind of pain. — Bob Dylan
If you don't have time to do it right, when will you have time to do it over? — John Wooden
Strategy is a commodity, execution is an art. — Peter Drucker
If you're afraid - don't do it, - if you're doing it - don't be afraid! — Genghis Khan
The most important thing is transforming our minds, for a new way of thinking, a new outlook: we should strive to develop a new inner world. — Dalai Lama
The only journey is the one within. — Rainer Maria Rilke
Excellence is not a skill. It is an attitude. — Ralph Marston
A man with outward courage dares to die: a man with inner courage dares to live. — Lao Tzu
It is not fair to ask of others what you are unwilling to do yourself. — Eleanor Roosevelt
Sometimes success isn't about making the right decision, it's more about making some decision. — Robin Sharma
When in doubt, don't. — Benjamin Franklin
An ounce of emotion is equal to a ton of facts. — John Junor
If you smile when no one else is around, you really mean it. — Andy Rooney
The road of excess leads to the palace of wisdom. — William Blake
Small opportunities are often the beginning of great enterprises. — Demosthenes
There are no ordinary moments. — Dan Millman
Trust your hunches. They're usually based on facts filed away just below the conscious level. — Joyce Brothers
Time is more valuable than money. You can get more money, but you cannot get more time. — Jim Rohn
The purpose of life is to live it, to taste experience to the utmost, to reach out eagerly and without fear for newer and richer experience. — Eleanor Roosevelt
This is the whole point of technology. It creates an appetite for immortality on the one hand. It threatens universal extinction on the other. Technology is lust removed from nature. — Don DeLillo
The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams. — Colin R. Davis
Love is blind; friendship closes its eyes. — Friedrich Nietzsche
Before you put on a frown, make absolutely sure there are no smiles available. — James M. Beggs
One must be fond of people and trust them if one is not to make a mess of life. — E. M. Forster
The goal is not to be perfect by the end, the goal is to be better today. — Simon Sinek
I can never decide whether my dreams are the result of my thoughts or my thoughts the result of my dreams. — D. H. Lawrence
Never explain - your friends do not need it and your enemies will not believe you anyway. — Elbert Hubbard
Successful people tend to become more successful because they are always thinking about their successes. — Brian Tracy
The virtue of justice consists in moderation, as regulated by wisdom. — Aristotle
He who knows, does not speak. He who speaks, does not know. — Laozi
You can't climb the ladder of success with your hands in your pockets. — Arnold Schwarzenegger
Progress lies not in enhancing what is, but in advancing toward what will be. — Kahlil Gibran
The way you see people is the way you treat them, and the way you treat them is what they become. — Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
When at last you are come to the ocean of happiness, do not go back thirsty. — Kabir
To see things in the seed, that is genius. — Laozi
Happiness cannot be travelled to, owned, earned, worn or consumed. Happiness is the spiritual experience of living every minute with love, grace and gratitude. — Denis Waitley
If you're afraid to die, you're afraid to live. You can't have one without the other. — Rita Mae Brown
It's okay to be discouraged. It's not okay to quit. — Ryan Holiday
The more something threatens your identity, the more you will avoid it. — Mark Manson
When you get up in the morning, you have two choices - either to be happy or to be unhappy. Just choose to be happy. — Norman Vincent Peale
We are all something, but none of us are everything. — Blaise Pascal
What is planted in each person's soul will sprout. — Rumi
Habit, if not resisted, soon becomes necessity. — Augustine of Hippo
The first half of life is devoted to forming a healthy ego, the second half is going inward and letting go of it. — Carl Jung
The superior man acts before he speaks, and afterwards speaks according to his action. — Confucius
Some people thrive on huge, dramatic change. Some people prefer the slow and steady route. Do what's right for you. — Julie Morgenstern
Be mindful. Be grateful. Be positive. Be true. Be kind. — Roy T. Bennett
Even when a friend does something you do not like, he continues to be your friend. — Genghis Khan
The difference between what we do and what we are capable of doing would suffice to solve most of the world's problems. — Mahatma Gandhi
Have the courage to be ignorant of a great number of things, in order to avoid the calamity of being ignorant of everything. — Sydney Smith
Who looks outside, dreams, who looks inside, awakes. — Carl Jung
Do the one thing you think you cannot do. Fail at it. Try again. Do better the second time. The only people who never tumble are those who never mount the high wire. This is your moment. Own it. — Oprah Winfrey
One today is worth two tomorrows. — Benjamin Franklin
Time is the most valuable thing a man can spend. — Theophrastus
The great myth of our times is that technology is communication. — Libby Larsen
Adversity causes some men to break, others to break records. — William Arthur Ward
You're never too old for anything. — Betty White
Your life does not get better by chance, it gets better by change. — Jim Rohn
However difficult life may seem, there is always something you can do and succeed at. — Stephen Hawking
Freeing oneself from words is liberation. — Bodhidharma
Tennis is a perfect combination of violent action taking place in an atmosphere of total tranquillity. — Billie Jean King
It may happen sometimes that a long debate becomes the cause of a longer friendship. Commonly, those who dispute with one another at last agree. — Elbert Hubbard
Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake. — Napoleon
All difficult things have their origin in that which is easy, and great things in that which is small. — Laozi
Relax. Nothing is under control. — Adi Da Samraj
If I know what love is, it is because of you. — Hermann Hesse
Fear of death is fear of surrender to Infinity. Learn to surrender, to exist at Infinity while alive, and fear of death dissolves. — Adi Da Samraj
The winner ain't the one with the fastest car it's the one who refuses to lose. — Dale Earnhardt
Technology frightens me to death. It's designed by engineers to impress other engineers. And they always come with instruction booklets that are written by engineers for other engineers — which is why almost no technology ever works. — John Cleese
Out of your vulnerabilities will come your strength. — Sigmund Freud
